projects.title: Shared Preferences App
projects.description:
The Shared Preferences App is a lightweight mobile application developed to demonstrate efficient local data storage using key-value pairs. It enables users to store, retrieve, update, and delete small amounts of persistent data such as user settings, preferences, and session information.

The application focuses on simplicity, performance, and usability, making it ideal for managing small-scale data without the need for a full database system.
projects.date: Role  Mobile App Developer (Flutter / Android),Lightweight Local Storage App using Key-Value Persistence
projects.fullDetails:
What is Shared Preferences?

Shared Preferences is a local storage mechanism used in mobile development (primarily Android and Flutter) that allows applications to store small amounts of data persistently in the form of key-value pairs.

How the Application Works
Users input data such as username, email, or app settings
The data is stored locally using SharedPreferences
Stored data can be retrieved and displayed instantly
Users can update or delete stored data as needed
Data remains available even after restarting the application
Architecture Overview
Frontend UI handles user interaction
SharedPreferences API manages local storage
State updates reflect instantly in the UI
Key Features
Persistent local data storage
Fast read and write operations
Simple key-value based data handling
Clean and user-friendly interface
Real-time updates of stored data
No dependency on external databases
Efficient memory usage
Easy integration into larger applications

projects.title: Calculator_App
projects.description: The Calculator App is a native-compiled mobile application developed with Flutter and Dart. It provides users with a fast, intuitive, and reliable tool for everyday arithmetic calculations. By leveraging Flutter's single codebase architecture, the app ensures a consistent experience and smooth performance across both iOS and Android devices.
